title: Larr Haverel
content:
{{Top|leg}} {{Character |type=Galactic Empire |image=[[File:Larr_Haverel.jpg]] |name=Larr Haverel |homeworld= |birth= |death= |species=[[Human/Legends|Human]] |gender=[[Gender/Legends|Male]] |pronouns=new |height= |mass= |hair=Brown |feathers= |eyes=Brown |skin= |cyber= |families=new |parents=new |partners=new |siblings=new |children=new |affiliation=*[[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] **[[Imperial Army/Legends|Imperial Army]] |masters= |apprentices= }} {{Quote|I'm suggesting that the Empire and our oaths have been betrayed. And I'm suggesting that it's our duty to set things right.|Larr Haverel to [[Dagon Niriz]]|{{JournalCite|11|Command Decision}}}} '''Larr Haverel''' was an [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[General/Legends|general]] who was dedicated to the welfare of his men and suspicious of aliens. He was assigned to the [[Star Destroyer/Legends|Star Destroyer]] ''[[Admonitor]]'' as commander of the [[Imperial Army/Legends|Imperial Army]] infantry complement on [[Grand Admiral/Legends|Grand Admiral]] [[Mitth'raw'nuruodo/Legends|Thrawn's]] mapping expedition to the [[Unknown Regions/Legends|Unknown Regions]]. He was thoroughly unenthusiastic about his mission, seeing it as exile and political punishment for Thrawn that unfairly impacted his career. ==Biography== {{Quote|Such as maybe he's made a private deal with this Creysis pirate. ''[…]''''"
"''You must be joking.''"
"''Am I? ''[…]'' Look at the facts. Thrawn agrees to send a contingent to talk to Creysis; but instead of sending it right away, he holds off for three hours. Meanwhile, he has the Zeta shuttle and one of the TIE fighters locked away in the Number Six maintenance area with about fifty techs swarming all over them.|Larr Haverel and Dagon Niriz|}} When Thrawn put several [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtroopers]] at risk in a complex game of strategy against the warlord [[Creysis]] without informing Haverel of the true depth of his plans, Haverel mistook Thrawn's tactics for mistakes. As Thrawn seemingly continued to do nothing, Haverel suspected incompetence and finally treachery on his commander's part. He approached the captain of the ''Admonitor'', [[Dagon Niriz]], to seek support for removing Thrawn from command and intervening against Creysis. While also deeply unsatisfied with Thrawn's performance, Niriz was uneasy with the prospect of removing him from command, which he regarded as mutiny. Unable to get Niriz's support, Haverel departed. He returned not long after, however, with six troopers in tow. Striding onto the bridge, he requested that Thrawn return to his quarters under guard and turn command of the ship over to Niriz. Calmly, Thrawn asked him what he proposed to do to rescue the men aboard Creysis's ship. Haverel was unable to provide a strong alternative, but insisted that taking the fight to Creysis was the only responsible way to go forward, though it would expose the men aboard to danger. Thrawn disagreed, and in a crucial moment, Niriz refused Haverel his support. Determined, Haverel raised his arm, presumably to attack or order an attack, but at that moment the break Thrawn had been waiting for arrived, and Thrawn and Niriz sprang into action. Haverel merely watched as the admiral efficiently [[Skirmish between the Admonitor and Creysis's scout squadron|dealt]] with Creysis. Nevertheless, Haverel had failed Thrawn's test of faith. He was sent back to the [[Capital/Legends|capital]] [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]] for court-martial. ==Appearances== *{{JournalCite|11|Command Decision|reprint=1}} {{1st}} ==Sources== *''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' *''[[The Essential Reader's Companion]]'' ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Havarel, Larr}} [[Category:Humans]] [[Category:Imperial Army generals of the Galactic Empire]] [[Category:Males]]
